result:
Done. Wrote **`adr-001.md`** — a standalone ~2.5-page ADR with all five required sections.

**What it commits to**, drawing the threads of the roundtable together:

- **Decision:** Deliver EU residency as a **regional cell** — a full second copy of the monolith in `eu-central`, tenants pinned to region at signup, **no cross-region sync**, thin shared control plane. Resolved the live Winston-vs-Amelia/Mary fault line (whole-cell vs. PII-only carve-out) **in favor of the cell**, because the carve-out would put an unscoped 140k-line PII-tagging audit on the critical path of a non-negotiable deadline.
- **Options:** (A) regional cells [chosen], (B) narrow PII-only carve-out, (C) split the monolith — each with concrete pros/cons.
- **Deferred & decoupled:** monolith split parked (no customer behind it); APAC treated as a measured front-end latency spike first, infra only if data-distance is proven the cause.
- **Consequences** spell out the parts that hurt — doubled on-call surface, partitioned data breaking cross-region reporting, a new control-plane seam, drift risk — and separate what we **accept** (whole-tenant residency, region fixed at signup) from what we **defer** (split, PII classification, APAC infra/cell).
- **Revisit triggers** are specific and signal-based, including a dated mid-build checkpoint (~2026-08-10).

Assumptions are tagged `[ASSUMPTION]` (APAC root-cause, budget headroom, third-region cost). Ready to take to the CEO.

That wraps the party. The four voices agreed on the deferrals and split cleanly on the EU mechanism — the ADR makes that call explicitly so the team isn't relitigating it next week. If you want a second round (e.g., Sally on booking-page UX during the cutover, or Paige to tighten the doc for the CEO audience), just say the word.
